examples:
!aws-security-lake-data-sources-list
aws-security-lake-data-lakes-list
#
Retrieves the Amazon Security Lake configuration object for the specified Amazon Web Services Regions.
In order to run this command the user must have 'securitylake' permissions.
Base Command
#
aws-security-lake-data-lakes-list

Command Example
#
!aws-security-lake-data-lakes-list

Amazon Web Services »
Configure Amazon Security Lake in Cortex

outputs.description: Retrieves the status of the configuration operation for an account in Amazon Security Lake. AWS.SecurityLake.dataLakeArn The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) created by you to provide to the subscriber.
outputs.name: AWS.SecurityLake.createStatus

outputs.description: The ID of the KMS encryption key used by Amazon Security Lake to encrypt the Security Lake object. AWS.SecurityLake.lifecycleConfiguration.expiration.days Number of days before data expires in the Amazon Security Lake object.
outputs.name: AWS.SecurityLake.encryptionConfiguration.kmsKeyId

outputs.description: Number of days before data transitions to a different S3 Storage Class in the Amazon Security Lake object. AWS.SecurityLake.lifecycleConfiguration.transitions.storageClass The range of storage classes that you can choose from based on the data access, resiliency, and cost requirements of your workloads.
outputs.name: AWS.SecurityLake.lifecycleConfiguration.transitions.days

outputs.description: The Amazon Web Services regions where Security Lake is enabled. AWS.SecurityLake.replicationConfiguration.regions Replication enables automatic, asynchronous copying of objects across Amazon S3 buckets.
outputs.name: AWS.SecurityLake.region

outputs.description: Replication settings for the Amazon S3 buckets. This parameter uses the Identity and Access Management (IAM) role you created that is managed by Security Lake, to ensure the replication setting is correct. AWS.SecurityLake.s3BucketArn The ARN for the Amazon Security Lake Amazon S3 bucket.
outputs.name: AWS.SecurityLake.replicationConfiguration.roleArn

outputs.description: The reason code for the exception of the last UpdateDataLake or DeleteDataLake API request. AWS.SecurityLake.updateStatus.exception.reason The reason for the exception of the last UpdateDataLake or DeleteDataLake API request.
outputs.name: AWS.SecurityLake.updateStatus.exception.code

outputs.description: The unique ID for the last UpdateDataLake or DeleteDataLake API request. AWS.SecurityLake.updateStatus.status The status of the last UpdateDataLake or DeleteDataLake API request that was requested.
outputs.name: AWS.SecurityLake.updateStatus.requestId
